Guilt and shame, says the allegory, were at first companions and in the beginning of the journey, inseparable kept together, but their union was soon found to be disargeeable and inconvenient to both. Guilt and shame frequently uneasiness and shame often betrayed secret conspiracies of guilt. After long disagreement, therefore, they at length consented to part forever guilt boldly walked alone to overtake fate, that went before in the shape of an executive, but shame being naturally timorous returned back to keep company with virtue, which in the beginning of their journey they had left behind. These children after men have traveled through a few stages in vice, shame fore sakes them and returns...... to wait upon the virtues they have still remaining
After long disagreement therefore, they at length consented to part forever in the passage means
options: They agreed to part since they consented
They argee to part because they were not happy together
They argeed to part because they had disagreed for a long time
